_description: Index of MusicXML files for Sound First curriculum. Each entry maps to a Material in the database.
_notes: This file stores HUMAN-CURATED metadata (teaching intent, source, category).
EXTRACTED metadata (actual notes, rhythms, intervals) comes from music21 analysis.
Run 'python -m app.seed_data' to sync to SQLite.

materials._comment: === TEMPLATE - Copy this entry for each new file ===
materials.title: Example Tune
materials._active_note: Set false to exclude from seeding without deleting

teaching_intent._note: What we WANT the student to learn from this material (human-curated)
teaching_intent._required_note: Capability IDs the user MUST have before attempting this (prerequisites)
teaching_intent._teaching_note: Capabilities we TEACH right before/alongside this piece (shown as mini-lessons)
teaching_intent._incidental_note: Capabilities present in the score but not our teaching focus (discovered by music21)

focus_cards._recommended_note: Focus card names that pair well with this material
focus_cards._always_note: Focus cards that should always be offered for this material

range._note_format: Scientific pitch notation: C4, Bb3, F#5. Used for user range filtering.

materials._largest_interval_note: Widest melodic interval in the piece (e.g., 'P5', 'M6', 'P8'). Used for progressive interval filtering. Materials are unlocked when user's max_melodic_interval >= this value.

transposition._disallowed_note: Keys that DON'T work for this piece (rare). Empty = all keys allowed.
transposition._original_note: The key as written in the source file

pitch_reference._TONAL_note: For pieces with a clear key center. Stores tonic and mode.
pitch_reference._ANCHOR_INTERVAL_note: For pattern-based materials without fixed key. Defines intervals from a movable anchor pitch. Example: 'Do-Re-Do' pattern = [0, +2, 0] semitones from any starting note. User hears it in different keys but the PATTERN is what matters.

interval_pattern._offsets_note: Semitone offsets from anchor. [0, 2, 0] = unison, M2 up, back to unison
interval_pattern._midi_note: MIDI number of the 'home' pitch in canonical version. 60 = C4

_extracted_by_music21._note: This section is AUTO-POPULATED by running the analyzer. Do not edit manually.
_extracted_by_music21._largest_interval_note: Widest melodic interval found in the score (e.g., 'P5')
